So security is a huge focus.

So we have three sort of pillars or, you know, buckets of core personas.
You know, the foundational one is developers, right?
Like, Clerk was specifically built for developers, you know, more specifically front-end or full-stack developers building with React.
You know, we have SDKs that support a number of different frameworks.
But, you know, at the end of the day, you know, React frameworks are the ones that we are most focused on because those are the ones that are most popular.
Those are the ones that, you know, most developers are building with.
The second cohort is the one that Brian mentioned.
It's early stages.
Startups, founders, could be CTOs, it could be a technical founder.

The third bucket should be no surprise, but it's AI builders.
And really, there's two different personas within that.

I don't think we've dug deep enough to really understand, you know, how, you know, further how they're using AI tooling.
Maybe Brian has some thoughts on that, but the flip side of the AI builder is the vibe coder, the non-technical audience that may have really strong business acumen and they're using...

You know, the likes of Lovable, Bolt, or V0 to, you know, help them get over that hump and, you know, build the actual application, especially if they are non-technical or don't have technical resources.
So in terms of, you know, why we lose customers or, you know, why we don't win customers, I would say there's a lot of reasons.
You know, the individual developer crowd, you know, really likes to scream about, you just open source alternatives.
There's a big belief that, you know, authentication is not something that you should be paying for.
And that, you know, it's, you know, if you spend the time, it's quite, you know, it's easier to roll your own authentication and manage it.
There's a cohort that believe that.
There's also the cohort that believes, you know, that, like I said, you shouldn't pay for authentication.
So, you know, there's open source.
There were alternatives that kind of give you more or less the framework, and then you can kind of customize it as you need, but more maintenance, but no costs associated there.

Another thing that we hear is the fact of data ownership, and I think this also goes back to the open source issue, is that, you know, Clerk manages the user data within our own database, but anybody that's using Clerk has the ability to download their data.

You know, we have really strong data privacy structure in place, so, you know, that's obviously a way to set their concerns at ease, but, you know, I think we all know and have heard about, you know, plenty of data security, data breach incidents where, you know, it just causes people to be more reluctant.
If they're not owning their own data, because obviously, if we have a data breach, they're going to be upset at us, but then all of their users are going to be upset at them in terms of that issue.

So other issues I would say, too, is we've had a lot of feedback around pricing, even though if you look at the competitive landscape, pricing is actually quite favorable in some respects to competitors.
We're going through a pricing rehaul now.
I don't really know the details of that just yet, but that is another reason that we see churn or migration.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Kick off engagement between Clerk and GrowthX to improve Clerk's content strategy and AI-driven growth initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Clerk seeks to leverage LLMs for growth, aiming to increase signups attributed to AI engines from 1% to 10%+](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [Key challenges: limited bandwidth (4-person team), difficulty proving blog ROI, and attribution issues with LLM-driven traffic](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=912.0)
  - [Focus areas: improving AEO/SEO, YouTube partnerships, open source collaborations, and B2B-focused content](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=787.0)

## Topics

### Clerk's Current Growth Initiatives

  - [Core channels: Organic search, YouTube partnerships, AEO, open source partnerships](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=2941.0)
  - [Supplementary channels: Paid social, newsletters, events, co-marketing with DevTools](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3071.0)
  - [Recent test: B2B SaaS multi-tenancy content series (generic to Clerk-specific)](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3302.0)
  - [Docs praised for comprehensiveness and ease of navigation](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3202.0)

### Competitive Landscape

  - [Main competitors: Auth0, WorkOS, Stytch, FusionAuth, Firebase](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=2526.0)
  - [Emerging threat: BetterAuth (open-source, growing "cult following")](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=2549.0)
  - [Differentiation: DX/UX focus, pre-built components, security emphasis](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3366.0)

### Customer Personas and Pain Points

  - [Three core personas: Developers (React-focused), early-stage startups, AI builders](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=1639.0)
  - [Pain points: Data ownership concerns, pricing objections, integration complexities](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=1756.0)
  - [Strength: Positive developer experience, comprehensive documentation](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3202.0)

### LLM Strategy and Challenges

  - [Inbound LLM traffic growing rapidly, but conversions lag (1% of signups)](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=694.0)
  - [Hypothesis: Decision-makers research via LLMs, delegate implementation](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=1453.0)
  - [Challenge: Proving ROI and attribution from LLM-driven traffic](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=1499.0)

### GrowthX Collaboration Approach

  - [Weekly recurring calls, product deep dives, and customer analysis planned](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3874.0)
  - [GrowthX to access Clerk's data (BigQuery, Hex, Elastic) for insights](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3712.0)
  - [Focus on dense learning period to inform content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3359.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Alex to share:](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3842.0)
      - [Gonto article detailing YouTube/open source partnership outreach](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3633.0)
      - [Content backlog for GrowthX review](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3750.0)
      - [Sample data exports from BigQuery/Hex/Elastic](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3750.0)
  - [GrowthX to:](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3712.0)
      - [Schedule product and customer deep dives](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3892.0)
      - [Begin research on search gaps, topic clusters, and content opportunities](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3359.0)
      - [Set up shared Google Drive for large file transfers](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3720.0)
  - [Both teams to determine best method for ongoing data/insight sharing](https://fathom.video/share/8PGnLqzxbVzmBFgy1WK1Mu1dbJEgb7dA?tab=summary&timestamp=3925.0)

action_items.description: Send GrowthX team article about YouTube partnerships process (from Gonta)
Share content backlog with GrowthX team
Provide GrowthX access to relevant data (BigQuery/Hex exports, Elastic sample data)
Send initial resources to GrowthX team before Thursday
Set up weekly recurring calls with Clerk team starting next week
Schedule product deep dive and customer deep dive meetings with Clerk team
